assessments.status_rationale: Today’s items describe active U.S.-Iran conflict dynamics (e.g., downed/rescued airman, Trump statements) but do not provide evidence that the FAA has issued a new/amended SFAR or formal flight restriction explicitly expanding coverage to a Gulf-state FIR/airspace area.
No news bullet indicates Maxar/Planet/BlackSky/Airbus publicly confirmed restricting or delaying Middle East imagery due to a U.S. government request/directive.

While several bullets reference the Iran war and the Strait of Hormuz, none show a named top-5 container carrier announcing a new Gulf/Hormuz security or war-risk surcharge explicitly citing conflict/security risk.
MarketWatch mentions surging gas prices amid the Iran war, but there is no cited EIA U.S. Gulf Coast kerosene-type jet fuel spot price data demonstrating a business-day print at or above $3.75/gal.
No bullet shows a UK MoD/FCDO/ministerial confirmation that U.S. combat operations related to the Iran conflict used RAF Lakenheath as a launch point or operational base.
Nothing in today’s news indicates the U.S. Senate has held a recorded confirmation vote confirming Kevin Warsh to a Fed leadership role.
No IRS ‘Filing Season Statistics’ weekly release data is provided in today’s items to verify whether the average refund is ≥10% higher year-over-year for the comparable week.

The bullets include Apple-adjacent/AI tooling items (e.g., an iPhone app for Gemma 4) but no Apple press release/keynote/support documentation announcing a new named third-party gen-AI model provider integrated into Siri/Apple Intelligence.
No bullet reports Moody’s or S&P taking a negative rating action on a publicly traded U.S. BDC or alternative-credit manager citing private-credit portfolio stress/refinancing/valuation uncertainty.
